title: ResolveResourcesReport
description:
Output ``data`` block for ``resolve-resources`` (WS5 #5).

Wraps inside the standard SuccessEnvelope's ``data`` field (see
envelope.json). Carries the three resolved fields plus a ``provenance``
map recording HOW each was resolved, so a caller can audit which values
were caller-supplied vs. auto-resolved vs. cold-start. ``walltime_sec``
and ``partition`` are nullable: a cold-start (no runtime prior) leaves
walltime null (the caller falls back to the cluster cold-start walltime),
and an absent partition config leaves partition null.

walltime_sec.title: Walltime Sec
walltime_sec.description: Resolved walltime (seconds), or ``null`` on cold-start (no runtime prior — the caller falls back to clusters.<cluster>.default_walltime_sec via get_default_walltime_sec).

gpu_type.title: Gpu Type
gpu_type.description: Resolved gpu_type, or ``null`` when the cluster declares no gpu_types and the caller supplied none.

partition.title: Partition
partition.description: Resolved partition, or ``null`` when no partition config was available (``no_partitions_supplied``) or recommend-partition found no safe partition.

provenance.title: Provenance
provenance.description: Per-field resolution provenance, so the caller can audit each value's source.

walltime_sec.title: Walltime Provenance
walltime_sec.description: ``caller`` (override), ``prior_p95`` (read-runtime-prior p95 × safety_mult), or a ``cold_start_*`` reason (no profile key, verb present but no samples, or verb absent/erroring).

partition.title: Partition Provenance
partition.description: ``caller`` (override), ``no_partitions_supplied`` (no partition config), or ``recommend_partition:<rationale>`` (delegated to recommend-partition, carrying its rationale e.g. ``debug_short_walltime``).
